response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which are not present in the underwater environment but the act of living underwater involves breathing which is not possible for humans in the same way as on land and requires the presence of oxygen in the air which is not available in liquid form  
Sec. 2  
the confusion may arise from the idea that underwater environments are isolated from allergens but this is not the case as humans cannot survive underwater without specialized equipment and even then the presence of allergens in the environment remains a concern and the primary issue is not the allergen but the inability to breathe underwater which makes long term survival impossible regardless of allergies
